# Fixture Design Skill

This synthetic capture is not real skill text. It exists only to exercise the
adapter parser, registry builder, diff command, and stack advisor tests.

## Design Read

Start with a brief read that names the project type, audience, brand maturity,
and aesthetic direction. The fixture uses taste and brand language so the
coverage tags include taste direction.

- State the design read before code.
- Avoid generic AI style defaults.
- Use the brand signal as the deciding input.

## Dial Settings

The adapter should extract these two dials exactly.

- DESIGN_VARIANCE: 7
- MOTION_INTENSITY: 3

Keep VISUAL_DENSITY absent so tests can confirm partial dial extraction.

## Layout And Typography

Use a responsive grid, a disciplined hero, and sections that do not all share
the same layout. Typography should set a real type scale, keep headline lines
balanced, and maintain readable text measure.

- Hero content should fit without crowding.
- Grid sections should change rhythm.
- Typography choices should match the brand.

## Motion And Interaction

Motion is restrained in this fixture. Buttons still need hover, focus, and
press states. Transitions should respect reduced-motion preferences and avoid
layout thrash.

- Hover states communicate affordance.
- Focus states remain visible.
- Reduced-motion alternatives are present.

## Audit And Sources

The final pass checks accessibility, contrast, keyboard behavior, source
provenance, and license data. Registry output should keep paths relative and
report the capture URL, retrieved date, and license.

- Accessibility is strict.
- Audit findings are source-aware.
- The capture header is the provenance source.
